{"allowedPrompts": [{"tool": "Bash", "prompt": "run docker compose commands"}, {"tool": "Bash", "prompt": "run pytest and tests"}, {"tool": "Bash", "prompt": "run alembic migration commands"}, {"tool": "Bash", "prompt": "install python dependencies with poetry"}, {"tool": "Bash", "prompt": "run uvicorn dev server"}], "plan": "# Meridian Training Operations System — Backend Blueprint

## Context

This plan covers the full backend implementation for the Meridian Training Operations System: a production-grade, fully offline-capable training management platform. The backend serves five user roles (Administrator, Instructor, Learner, Finance Analyst, Data Ops) and orchestrates class scheduling, live/recorded session access, booking flows, offline checkout with promotions, staff search, payments, background jobs, and multi-source data ingestion — all running in a single `docker compose up` environment.

---

## 1. Tech Stack Summary

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | PyNest (wraps FastAPI) — Python 3.11+ |
| Database | PostgreSQL 15 |
| ORM | SQLAlchemy 2.x (async) + asyncpg |
| Migrations | Alembic |
| Auth/Crypto | Passlib (bcrypt), cryptography (Fernet) |
| Cache/Broker | Redis 7 |
| Background Jobs | Celery 5 + Celery Beat |
| Streaming Ingest | Kafka (confluent-kafka-python) |
| Monitoring | Prometheus + Grafana |
| Search | PostgreSQL Full-Text Search + pg_trgm |
| Testing | pytest + httpx (async) |
| Reverse Proxy | Nginx |
| Containerization | Docker + Docker Compose |

---

## 2. Repository Structure

```
repo/
├── backend/
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── pyproject.toml           # dependencies (Poetry)
│   ├── alembic/
│   │   ├── env.py
│   │   └── versions/            # migration scripts
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── main.py              # PyNest app bootstrap
│   │   ├── core/
│   │   │   ├── config.py        # Settings via pydantic-settings
│   │   │   ├── database.py      # async engine + session factory
│   │   │   ├── security.py      # password hash, token, lockout
│   │   │   ├── encryption.py    # Fernet field encryption/decryption
│   │   │   ├── audit.py         # audit log helpers
│   │   │   └── exceptions.py    # global exception handlers
│   │   ├── modules/
│   │   │   ├── auth/            # login, session, lockout
│   │   │   ├── users/           # CRUD, roles, masking
│   │   │   ├── locations/       # sites, rooms
│   │   │   ├── courses/         # course catalog
│   │   │   ├── instructors/     # instructor profiles
│   │   │   ├── sessions/        # scheduled sessions, recurring rules
│   │   │   ├── bookings/        # booking state machine
│   │   │   ├── attendance/      # check-in, stats
│   │   │   ├── replays/         # recorded sessions, access rules
│   │   │   ├── promotions/      # discount rules, stack controls
│   │   │   ├── checkout/        # cart, best-offer engine, receipts
│   │   │   ├── payments/        # ledger, callbacks, refunds, recon
│   │   │   ├── search/          # advanced search, saved searches
│   │   │   ├── ingestion/       # job configs, Kafka, CDC, batch
│   │   │   ├── jobs/            # Celery tasks + Beat schedules
│   │   │   └── monitoring/      # Prometheus metrics endpoint, alerts
│   │   └── shared/
│   │       ├── models/          # SQLAlchemy base models
│   │       ├── schemas/         # Pydantic request/response schemas
│   │       └── utils/           # pagination, csv export, timezone
├── nginx/
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   └── nginx.conf
├── monitoring/
│   ├── prometheus.yml
│   └── grafana/
│       └── provisioning/
├── docker-compose.yml
└── README.md
```

Each `modules/<name>/` follows PyNest module conventions:
```
<name>/
  ├── __init__.py
  ├── module.py          # PyNest module declaration
  ├── controller.py      # route handlers
  ├── service.py         # business logic
  ├── repository.py      # async DB queries
  ├── models.py          # SQLAlchemy ORM models
  └── schemas.py         # Pydantic I/O schemas
```

---

## 3. Database Schema & Entity Relationships

### Core Entities

**users**
- id (UUID PK), username (unique), password_hash, role (enum: admin/instructor/learner/finance/dataops)
- email_encrypted, phone_encrypted (Fernet at rest)
- is_active, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, last_login
- created_at, updated_at

**locations**
- id, name, address, timezone (IANA string), is_active

**rooms**
- id, location_id (FK), name, capacity, is_active

**courses**
- id, title, description, duration_minutes, category, is_active

**instructors**
- id, user_id (FK unique), bio, is_active

**sessions**
- id, course_id (FK), instructor_id (FK), room_id (FK)
- start_time (timestamptz), end_time (timestamptz)
- capacity, enrolled_count, buffer_minutes (default 10)
- status (enum: scheduled/live/completed/cancelled)
- recurrence_rule_id (FK nullable) — RRULE reference
- created_by (FK users), created_at, updated_at

**recurrence_rules**
- id, rrule_string (e.g., "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=TU,TH;BYHOUR=18;BYMINUTE=30")
- start_date, end_date, parent_session_id (FK)

**bookings**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- status (enum: pending/confirmed/rescheduled/cancelled/no_show)
- rescheduled_from_id (FK self nullable)
- policy_fee_flagged (bool), cancellation_reason
- confirmed_at, cancelled_at, created_at, updated_at

**attendance_records**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- joined_at, left_at, minutes_attended, was_late (bool)

**replay_access_rules**
- id, session_id (FK), rule_type (enrolled_only/public/custom)
- available_from (timestamptz), available_until (timestamptz)

**replay_views**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK), watched_seconds, completed (bool), viewed_at

**promotions**
- id, name, type (enum: pct_off/fixed_off/bogo), value (numeric)
- min_order_amount (nullable), applies_to (course_ids jsonb, or "all")
- stack_group (nullable), is_exclusive (bool), is_active
- valid_from, valid_until

**orders**
- id, learner_id (FK), status (enum: pending/paid/cancelled/refunded)
- subtotal, discount_total, total, currency
- created_at, paid_at, closed_at, expires_at (30-min auto-close)

**order_items**
- id, order_id (FK), session_id (FK), unit_price, quantity

**order_promotions** (applied promos per order)
- id, order_id (FK), promotion_id (FK), discount_amount, explanation (text)

**payments**
- id, order_id (FK), terminal_ref, amount, status (enum: pending/completed/failed)
- callback_received_at, signature_verified (bool), raw_callback (jsonb)

**refunds**
- id, payment_id (FK), requested_by (FK users), amount, reason
- status (enum: pending/approved/completed/rejected)
- created_at, processed_at

**reconciliation_exports**
- id, export_date (date), file_path, generated_at, row_count

**saved_searches**
- id, user_id (FK), name, filters_json, created_at, updated_at
- (max 20 per user — enforced at service layer)

**audit_logs**
- id, actor_id (FK users), entity_type (varchar), entity_id (uuid)
- action (varchar), old_value (jsonb), new_value (jsonb)
- ip_address, created_at
- (90-day retention enforced by Celery purge job)

**ingestion_sources**
- id, name, type (enum: kafka/logstash/batch_file/cdc_mysql/cdc_pg)
- config_encrypted (Fernet jsonb), collection_frequency_seconds
- concurrency_cap, is_active, last_run_at, last_status

**ingestion_runs**
- id, source_id (FK), started_at, finished_at, rows_ingested, status, error_detail

**job_executions** (scheduler monitoring)
- id, job_name, started_at, finished_at, status (success/failure/late), error_detail

### Key Indexes

```sql
-- Sessions calendar queries
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_start_time ON sessions(start_time);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_instructor ON sessions(instructor_id);

-- Booking lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_learner ON bookings(learner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_session ON bookings(session_id);

-- Full-text search on users
CREATE INDEX idx_users_fts ON users USING GIN(to_tsvector('english', username));

-- pg_trgm for fuzzy search
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
CREATE INDEX idx_users_phone_trgm ON users USING GIN(phone_encrypted gin_trgm_ops); -- on decrypted view
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_id_trgm ON orders USING GIN(CAST(id AS text) gin_trgm_ops);

-- Audit log retention query
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_created ON audit_logs(created_at);

-- Promotions validity
CREATE INDEX idx_promotions_valid ON promotions(valid_from, valid_until) WHERE is_active = TRUE;
```

## 4. Authentication & Authorization

### Auth Flow
- `POST /api/auth/login` — validate username + password (bcrypt verify), check lockout, return JWT (access + refresh)
- `POST /api/auth/refresh` — exchange refresh token
- `POST /api/auth/logout` — invalidate refresh token (Redis blocklist)
- Session inactivity: access token TTL = 30 minutes; refresh extends on activity
- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts → locked for 15 minutes (stored on `users.locked_until`)

### Password Policy (enforced in Pydantic validator)
- Minimum 12 characters
- At least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, one special character
- Checked via regex in `core/security.py`

### JWT
- Algorithm: HS256 (secret from env var, never hardcoded)
- Access token payload: `sub` (user_id), `role`, `exp`, `iat`
- Refresh tokens stored as hashed value in Redis with TTL

### Role-Based Access Control
- FastAPI dependency `require_role(*roles)` applied per router
- Role matrix:

| Endpoint Group | admin | instructor | learner | finance | dataops |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Locations/Rooms CRUD | ✓ | | | | |
| Sessions CRUD | ✓ | own only | | | |
| Bookings | ✓ | read | own only | read | |
| Checkout/Orders | ✓ | | own only | ✓ | |
| Payments/Refunds | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Promotions CRUD | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Search & Export | ✓ | ✓ | | ✓ | |
| Ingestion Config | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Job Monitoring | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Audit Logs | ✓ | | | | |

## 6. Booking State Machine

```
PENDING ──────────────────────────────► CONFIRMED
    │                                       │
    │                              ┌────────┴────────┐
    │                              ▼                 ▼
    │                        RESCHEDULED         CANCELLED
    │                              │                 │
    └──────────────────────────────►      (terminal: NO_SHOW from CONFIRMED)
```

**Rules enforced in `bookings/service.py`:**
- Reschedule blocked if `session.start_time - now < 2 hours`
- Cancellation within 24 hours → `policy_fee_flagged = True`
- Cancellation decrements `sessions.enrolled_count`
- All state transitions appended to `audit_logs`

---

## 7. Best-Offer Checkout Engine (`checkout/service.py`)

```
Input: order_items (session_ids + quantities)
Step 1: Load all active promotions valid now for the order's courses
Step 2: Group by stack_group; is_exclusive promos form their own group
Step 3: For each valid combination:
   - Apply eligible promos
   - Compute total discount
Step 4: Select combination with maximum discount (tie-break: fewest promos)
Step 5: Build explanation list:
   [{"promo": "10% off", "amount": 15.00, "reason": "10% discount applied to Workshop A"}]
Step 6: Persist order_promotions rows with explanation text
```

Stack controls:
- `is_exclusive=True` → that promo cannot combine with any other
- Same `stack_group` → mutually exclusive (only one from group applied)
- Different groups / null group → stackable

---

## 8. Payment & Reconciliation

### Offline Payment Ledger Flow
1. Order confirmed → `payments` row created (status=pending), order `expires_at = now + 30min`
2. Celery Beat task every 1 min: close unpaid orders past `expires_at`
3. LAN terminal POSTs callback to `/api/payments/callback`
4. Signature verification: `HMAC-SHA256(secret_key, terminal_ref + amount + timestamp)` — reject if invalid
5. Payment status → completed, order status → paid, `paid_at` set

### Refund Flow
Finance analyst → `POST /api/refunds` → status=pending → admin/finance approve → status=completed → payment ledger updated

### Daily Reconciliation Export
- Celery Beat: `0 2 * * *` → `jobs/tasks.py::generate_reconciliation_export`
- Query: all completed payments for previous calendar day
- Write CSV to `EXPORTS_DIR/reconciliation_YYYY-MM-DD.csv`
- Insert row in `reconciliation_exports`

---

## 9. Background Jobs (Celery + Beat)

All tasks defined in `modules/jobs/tasks.py`:

| Task | Schedule | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `attendance_rollup` | Every 5 min | Aggregate session attendance stats |
| `close_expired_orders` | Every 1 min | Auto-close unpaid orders past 30 min |
| `generate_reconciliation_export` | 2:00 AM daily | Finance CSV export |
| `purge_audit_logs` | 3:00 AM daily | Delete audit_logs older than 90 days |
| `run_ingestion_jobs` | Configurable per source | Trigger enabled ingestion sources |
| `check_job_health` | Every 2 min | Alert if failure rate > 2% in last hour or job late > 10 min |
| `replay_access_expiry` | Every 10 min | Mark expired replay rules as inactive |

**Retry policy:** `max_retries=3`, `retry_backoff=True`, `retry_backoff_max=300` (5 min cap)

**Monitoring:** Each task wraps execution in try/finally, writes to `job_executions`, increments Prometheus counters (`job_success_total`, `job_failure_total`, `job_duration_seconds`).

---

## 10. Data Ingestion Pipeline

### Source Types (`modules/ingestion/`)

**Kafka** (`ingestion/adapters/kafka_adapter.py`)
- `confluent_kafka.Consumer` subscribing to configured topics
- Batch consume up to `concurrency_cap` messages per cycle
- Deserialize (JSON/Avro), validate against source schema, upsert via deterministic key

**Logstash / Flume (HTTP push)**
- Ingestion endpoint: `POST /api/ingestion/webhook/{source_id}` authenticated by source API key
- Validate, queue to Celery for async processing

**Batch File Import**
- Watched directory (Docker volume mount) or upload endpoint
- CSV/JSON parsing, schema validation with row-level error logging
- Deterministic reprocessing via file hash + row fingerprint (skip duplicates)

**CDC — MySQL/PostgreSQL**
- For MySQL: `mysql-connector-python` + binlog position tracking
- For PostgreSQL: logical replication slot via `psycopg2`
- Row-level upserts with `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`

### Connectivity Check
`POST /api/ingestion/sources/{id}/test-connection` → attempt connection, return latency + error if any

### Duplicate Prevention
Each source row hashed (source_id + natural key) → stored in `ingestion_dedup` Redis set with TTL = 7 days

---

## 11. Search Design

### Advanced Search (`POST /api/search`)

Filters accepted:
```json
{
  "invoice_number": "string",
  "learner_phone": "string",       // matched against masked/decrypted
  "enrollment_status": "confirmed",
  "date_range": {"from": "...", "to": "..."},
  "site_id": "uuid",
  "instructor_id": "uuid",
  "page": 1,
  "page_size": 50
}
```

Implementation:
- Base query on `bookings` joined with `sessions`, `users`, `locations`, `orders`
- Phone search: decrypt + substring match OR pg_trgm similarity
- Invoice number: trgm index on `CAST(orders.id AS text)`
- FTS fallback for keyword fields: `to_tsvector || plainto_tsquery`
- Pagination: keyset pagination (cursor on `created_at + id`) for performance
- "Results updated" feedback: response includes `total_count` + `query_time_ms`

### CSV/Excel Export
- `POST /api/search/export` runs same query, streams results in batches of 1000
- Hard cap: 50,000 rows → HTTP 400 if count exceeds
- CSV via Python `csv` module; Excel via `openpyxl`
- Returned as streaming response with `Content-Disposition` header

## 12. Sensitive Data Handling

### Field Encryption
`core/encryption.py` — Fernet symmetric encryption:
- Key loaded from env `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY`
- `encrypt_field(value: str) -> str` / `decrypt_field(value: str) -> str`
- Applied to: `users.email`, `users.phone`, `ingestion_sources.config`

### Masking in API Responses
- `users/schemas.py` `UserResponse` schema: phone shown as `+1-***-***-1234`
- Masking logic in `shared/utils/masking.py`
- Admin role: `UserDetailResponse` includes unmasked values

### Audit Logging
`core/audit.py::log_audit(actor_id, entity_type, entity_id, action, old_value, new_value, request)`  
Triggered on: session schedule changes, promotion changes, refund actions, access control changes

## 13. Monitoring & Alerting

### Prometheus Metrics (exposed at `/api/monitoring/metrics`)
- `http_request_duration_seconds` (histogram, by route + status)
- `job_success_total` / `job_failure_total` (counter, by job_name)
- `job_duration_seconds` (histogram)
- `active_sessions_gauge`
- `pending_orders_gauge`
- `ingestion_rows_total` (counter, by source)

### Alert Rules (evaluated in `jobs/tasks.py::check_job_health`)
- Failure rate > 2% over last 60 min → insert `monitoring_alerts` row + log WARNING
- Job late by > 10 min → insert alert + log WARNING
- Alerts surfaced via `GET /api/monitoring/alerts`

### Grafana
- Pre-provisioned dashboard JSON in `monitoring/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/`
- Datasource: Prometheus at `http://prometheus:9090`

## 15. Dockerization Plan

### Services in `docker-compose.yml`

| Service | Image | Port |
|---|---|---|

| `prometheus` | prom/prometheus:latest | 9090 |

### Backend Dockerfile
```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./
RUN pip install poetry && poetry install --no-root --no-dev
COPY . .
RUN poetry install --no-dev
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
```

### Startup Sequence (depends_on + healthchecks)
```
zookeeper → kafka
db (healthcheck: pg_isready) → backend → celery_worker, celery_beat
redis → backend → celery_worker, celery_beat
backend → nginx
prometheus → grafana
```

### Entrypoint Script (`docker-entrypoint.sh`)
1. Wait for DB healthcheck
2. Run `alembic upgrade head`
3. Seed default admin user if `users` table is empty
4. Start uvicorn

### Volumes
```yaml
volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:
  exports_data:         # reconciliation + search exports
  grafana_data:
  prometheus_data:
```

## 16. Database Migrations Strategy

- `alembic/env.py` imports all SQLAlchemy models for autogenerate
- Each migration: forward + downgrade
- Initial migration: all tables + indexes + extensions (pg_trgm, uuid-ossp)
- Subsequent migrations: additive only (no column drops without transition period)
- Run in entrypoint before app start

---

## 17. Testing Strategy

### Structure
```
backend/tests/
├── conftest.py         # async test DB, fixture factories, test client
├── unit/
│   ├── test_security.py
│   ├── test_best_offer.py
│   ├── test_booking_state.py
│   └── test_encryption.py
└── api/
    ├── test_auth.py
    ├── test_sessions.py
    ├── test_bookings.py
    ├── test_checkout.py
    ├── test_payments.py
    ├── test_search.py
    └── test_ingestion.py
```

### Approach
- **Unit tests**: pure logic (best-offer engine, booking rule checks, password validator, Fernet encrypt/decrypt)
- **API tests**: `httpx.AsyncClient` against test app instance with a real PostgreSQL test DB (separate DB, not mocked)
- **Fixtures**: `pytest-asyncio`, factory functions for users/sessions/bookings/orders
- **Test DB**: separate `meridian_test` database; `conftest.py` runs `alembic upgrade head` once, wraps each test in a transaction rollback
- **Coverage target**: 80%+ on service + controller layers

## Context

This plan covers the full backend implementation for the Meridian Training Operations System: a production-grade, fully offline-capable training management platform. The backend serves five user roles (Administrator, Instructor, Learner, Finance Analyst, Data Ops) and orchestrates class scheduling, live/recorded session access, booking flows, offline checkout with promotions, staff search, payments, background jobs, and multi-source data ingestion — all running in a single `docker compose up` environment.

## 1. Tech Stack Summary

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | PyNest (wraps FastAPI) — Python 3.11+ |
| Database | PostgreSQL 15 |
| ORM | SQLAlchemy 2.x (async) + asyncpg |
| Migrations | Alembic |
| Auth/Crypto | Passlib (bcrypt), cryptography (Fernet) |
| Cache/Broker | Redis 7 |
| Background Jobs | Celery 5 + Celery Beat |
| Streaming Ingest | Kafka (confluent-kafka-python) |
| Monitoring | Prometheus + Grafana |
| Search | PostgreSQL Full-Text Search + pg_trgm |
| Testing | pytest + httpx (async) |
| Reverse Proxy | Nginx |
| Containerization | Docker + Docker Compose |

│   │   └── shared/
│   │       ├── models/          # SQLAlchemy base models
│   │       ├── schemas/         # Pydantic request/response schemas
│   │       └── utils/           # pagination, csv export, timezone
├── nginx/
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   └── nginx.conf
├── monitoring/
│   ├── prometheus.yml
│   └── grafana/
│       └── provisioning/
├── docker-compose.yml
└── README.md
```

Each `modules/<name>/` follows PyNest module conventions:
```
<name>/
  ├── __init__.py
  ├── module.py          # PyNest module declaration
  ├── controller.py      # route handlers
  ├── service.py         # business logic
  ├── repository.py      # async DB queries
  ├── models.py          # SQLAlchemy ORM models
  └── schemas.py         # Pydantic I/O schemas
```

---

## 3. Database Schema & Entity Relationships

### Core Entities

**users**
- id (UUID PK), username (unique), password_hash, role (enum: admin/instructor/learner/finance/dataops)
- email_encrypted, phone_encrypted (Fernet at rest)
- is_active, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, last_login
- created_at, updated_at

**locations**
- id, name, address, timezone (IANA string), is_active

**rooms**
- id, location_id (FK), name, capacity, is_active

**courses**
- id, title, description, duration_minutes, category, is_active

**instructors**
- id, user_id (FK unique), bio, is_active

**sessions**
- id, course_id (FK), instructor_id (FK), room_id (FK)
- start_time (timestamptz), end_time (timestamptz)
- capacity, enrolled_count, buffer_minutes (default 10)
- status (enum: scheduled/live/completed/cancelled)
- recurrence_rule_id (FK nullable) — RRULE reference
- created_by (FK users), created_at, updated_at

**recurrence_rules**
- id, rrule_string (e.g., "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=TU,TH;BYHOUR=18;BYMINUTE=30")
- start_date, end_date, parent_session_id (FK)

**bookings**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- status (enum: pending/confirmed/rescheduled/cancelled/no_show)
- rescheduled_from_id (FK self nullable)
- policy_fee_flagged (bool), cancellation_reason
- confirmed_at, cancelled_at, created_at, updated_at

**attendance_records**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- joined_at, left_at, minutes_attended, was_late (bool)

**replay_access_rules**
- id, session_id (FK), rule_type (enrolled_only/public/custom)
- available_from (timestamptz), available_until (timestamptz)

**replay_views**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK), watched_seconds, completed (bool), viewed_at

**promotions**
- id, name, type (enum: pct_off/fixed_off/bogo), value (numeric)
- min_order_amount (nullable), applies_to (course_ids jsonb, or "all")
- stack_group (nullable), is_exclusive (bool), is_active
- valid_from, valid_until

**orders**
- id, learner_id (FK), status (enum: pending/paid/cancelled/refunded)
- subtotal, discount_total, total, currency
- created_at, paid_at, closed_at, expires_at (30-min auto-close)

**order_items**
- id, order_id (FK), session_id (FK), unit_price, quantity

**order_promotions** (applied promos per order)
- id, order_id (FK), promotion_id (FK), discount_amount, explanation (text)

**payments**
- id, order_id (FK), terminal_ref, amount, status (enum: pending/completed/failed)
- callback_received_at, signature_verified (bool), raw_callback (jsonb)

**refunds**
- id, payment_id (FK), requested_by (FK users), amount, reason
- status (enum: pending/approved/completed/rejected)
- created_at, processed_at

**reconciliation_exports**
- id, export_date (date), file_path, generated_at, row_count

**saved_searches**
- id, user_id (FK), name, filters_json, created_at, updated_at
- (max 20 per user — enforced at service layer)

**audit_logs**
- id, actor_id (FK users), entity_type (varchar), entity_id (uuid)
- action (varchar), old_value (jsonb), new_value (jsonb)
- ip_address, created_at
- (90-day retention enforced by Celery purge job)

**ingestion_sources**
- id, name, type (enum: kafka/logstash/batch_file/cdc_mysql/cdc_pg)
- config_encrypted (Fernet jsonb), collection_frequency_seconds
- concurrency_cap, is_active, last_run_at, last_status

**ingestion_runs**
- id, source_id (FK), started_at, finished_at, rows_ingested, status, error_detail

**job_executions** (scheduler monitoring)
- id, job_name, started_at, finished_at, status (success/failure/late), error_detail

### Key Indexes

```sql
-- Sessions calendar queries
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_start_time ON sessions(start_time);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_instructor ON sessions(instructor_id);

-- Booking lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_learner ON bookings(learner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_session ON bookings(session_id);

-- Full-text search on users
CREATE INDEX idx_users_fts ON users USING GIN(to_tsvector('english', username));

-- pg_trgm for fuzzy search
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
CREATE INDEX idx_users_phone_trgm ON users USING GIN(phone_encrypted gin_trgm_ops); -- on decrypted view
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_id_trgm ON orders USING GIN(CAST(id AS text) gin_trgm_ops);

-- Audit log retention query
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_created ON audit_logs(created_at);

-- Promotions validity
CREATE INDEX idx_promotions_valid ON promotions(valid_from, valid_until) WHERE is_active = TRUE;
```

---

## 4. Authentication & Authorization

### Auth Flow
- `POST /api/auth/login` — validate username + password (bcrypt verify), check lockout, return JWT (access + refresh)
- `POST /api/auth/refresh` — exchange refresh token
- `POST /api/auth/logout` — invalidate refresh token (Redis blocklist)
- Session inactivity: access token TTL = 30 minutes; refresh extends on activity
- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts → locked for 15 minutes (stored on `users.locked_until`)

### Password Policy (enforced in Pydantic validator)
- Minimum 12 characters
- At least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, one special character
- Checked via regex in `core/security.py`

### JWT
- Algorithm: HS256 (secret from env var, never hardcoded)
- Access token payload: `sub` (user_id), `role`, `exp`, `iat`
- Refresh tokens stored as hashed value in Redis with TTL

### Role-Based Access Control
- FastAPI dependency `require_role(*roles)` applied per router
- Role matrix:

| Endpoint Group | admin | instructor | learner | finance | dataops |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Locations/Rooms CRUD | ✓ | | | | |
| Sessions CRUD | ✓ | own only | | | |
| Bookings | ✓ | read | own only | read | |
| Checkout/Orders | ✓ | | own only | ✓ | |
| Payments/Refunds | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Promotions CRUD | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Search & Export | ✓ | ✓ | | ✓ | |
| Ingestion Config | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Job Monitoring | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Audit Logs | ✓ | | | | |

## 6. Booking State Machine

```
PENDING ──────────────────────────────► CONFIRMED
    │                                       │
    │                              ┌────────┴────────┐
    │                              ▼                 ▼
    │                        RESCHEDULED         CANCELLED
    │                              │                 │
    └──────────────────────────────►      (terminal: NO_SHOW from CONFIRMED)
```

**Rules enforced in `bookings/service.py`:**
- Reschedule blocked if `session.start_time - now < 2 hours`
- Cancellation within 24 hours → `policy_fee_flagged = True`
- Cancellation decrements `sessions.enrolled_count`
- All state transitions appended to `audit_logs`

---

## 7. Best-Offer Checkout Engine (`checkout/service.py`)

```
Input: order_items (session_ids + quantities)
Step 1: Load all active promotions valid now for the order's courses
Step 2: Group by stack_group; is_exclusive promos form their own group
Step 3: For each valid combination:
   - Apply eligible promos
   - Compute total discount
Step 4: Select combination with maximum discount (tie-break: fewest promos)
Step 5: Build explanation list:
   [{"promo": "10% off", "amount": 15.00, "reason": "10% discount applied to Workshop A"}]
Step 6: Persist order_promotions rows with explanation text
```

Stack controls:
- `is_exclusive=True` → that promo cannot combine with any other
- Same `stack_group` → mutually exclusive (only one from group applied)
- Different groups / null group → stackable

---

## 8. Payment & Reconciliation

### Offline Payment Ledger Flow
1. Order confirmed → `payments` row created (status=pending), order `expires_at = now + 30min`
2. Celery Beat task every 1 min: close unpaid orders past `expires_at`
3. LAN terminal POSTs callback to `/api/payments/callback`
4. Signature verification: `HMAC-SHA256(secret_key, terminal_ref + amount + timestamp)` — reject if invalid
5. Payment status → completed, order status → paid, `paid_at` set

### Refund Flow
Finance analyst → `POST /api/refunds` → status=pending → admin/finance approve → status=completed → payment ledger updated

### Daily Reconciliation Export
- Celery Beat: `0 2 * * *` → `jobs/tasks.py::generate_reconciliation_export`
- Query: all completed payments for previous calendar day
- Write CSV to `EXPORTS_DIR/reconciliation_YYYY-MM-DD.csv`
- Insert row in `reconciliation_exports`

---

## 9. Background Jobs (Celery + Beat)

All tasks defined in `modules/jobs/tasks.py`:

| Task | Schedule | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `attendance_rollup` | Every 5 min | Aggregate session attendance stats |
| `close_expired_orders` | Every 1 min | Auto-close unpaid orders past 30 min |
| `generate_reconciliation_export` | 2:00 AM daily | Finance CSV export |
| `purge_audit_logs` | 3:00 AM daily | Delete audit_logs older than 90 days |
| `run_ingestion_jobs` | Configurable per source | Trigger enabled ingestion sources |
| `check_job_health` | Every 2 min | Alert if failure rate > 2% in last hour or job late > 10 min |
| `replay_access_expiry` | Every 10 min | Mark expired replay rules as inactive |

**Retry policy:** `max_retries=3`, `retry_backoff=True`, `retry_backoff_max=300` (5 min cap)

**Monitoring:** Each task wraps execution in try/finally, writes to `job_executions`, increments Prometheus counters (`job_success_total`, `job_failure_total`, `job_duration_seconds`).

---

## 10. Data Ingestion Pipeline

### Source Types (`modules/ingestion/`)

**Kafka** (`ingestion/adapters/kafka_adapter.py`)
- `confluent_kafka.Consumer` subscribing to configured topics
- Batch consume up to `concurrency_cap` messages per cycle
- Deserialize (JSON/Avro), validate against source schema, upsert via deterministic key

**Logstash / Flume (HTTP push)**
- Ingestion endpoint: `POST /api/ingestion/webhook/{source_id}` authenticated by source API key
- Validate, queue to Celery for async processing

**Batch File Import**
- Watched directory (Docker volume mount) or upload endpoint
- CSV/JSON parsing, schema validation with row-level error logging
- Deterministic reprocessing via file hash + row fingerprint (skip duplicates)

**CDC — MySQL/PostgreSQL**
- For MySQL: `mysql-connector-python` + binlog position tracking
- For PostgreSQL: logical replication slot via `psycopg2`
- Row-level upserts with `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`

### Connectivity Check
`POST /api/ingestion/sources/{id}/test-connection` → attempt connection, return latency + error if any

### Duplicate Prevention
Each source row hashed (source_id + natural key) → stored in `ingestion_dedup` Redis set with TTL = 7 days

---

## 11. Search Design

### Advanced Search (`POST /api/search`)

Filters accepted:
```json
{
  "invoice_number": "string",
  "learner_phone": "string",       // matched against masked/decrypted
  "enrollment_status": "confirmed",
  "date_range": {"from": "...", "to": "..."},
  "site_id": "uuid",
  "instructor_id": "uuid",
  "page": 1,
  "page_size": 50
}
```

Implementation:
- Base query on `bookings` joined with `sessions`, `users`, `locations`, `orders`
- Phone search: decrypt + substring match OR pg_trgm similarity
- Invoice number: trgm index on `CAST(orders.id AS text)`
- FTS fallback for keyword fields: `to_tsvector || plainto_tsquery`
- Pagination: keyset pagination (cursor on `created_at + id`) for performance
- "Results updated" feedback: response includes `total_count` + `query_time_ms`

### CSV/Excel Export
- `POST /api/search/export` runs same query, streams results in batches of 1000
- Hard cap: 50,000 rows → HTTP 400 if count exceeds
- CSV via Python `csv` module; Excel via `openpyxl`
- Returned as streaming response with `Content-Disposition` header

---

## 12. Sensitive Data Handling

### Field Encryption
`core/encryption.py` — Fernet symmetric encryption:
- Key loaded from env `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY`
- `encrypt_field(value: str) -> str` / `decrypt_field(value: str) -> str`
- Applied to: `users.email`, `users.phone`, `ingestion_sources.config`

### Masking in API Responses
- `users/schemas.py` `UserResponse` schema: phone shown as `+1-***-***-1234`
- Masking logic in `shared/utils/masking.py`
- Admin role: `UserDetailResponse` includes unmasked values

### Audit Logging
`core/audit.py::log_audit(actor_id, entity_type, entity_id, action, old_value, new_value, request)`  
Triggered on: session schedule changes, promotion changes, refund actions, access control changes

---

## 13. Monitoring & Alerting

### Prometheus Metrics (exposed at `/api/monitoring/metrics`)
- `http_request_duration_seconds` (histogram, by route + status)
- `job_success_total` / `job_failure_total` (counter, by job_name)
- `job_duration_seconds` (histogram)
- `active_sessions_gauge`
- `pending_orders_gauge`
- `ingestion_rows_total` (counter, by source)

### Alert Rules (evaluated in `jobs/tasks.py::check_job_health`)
- Failure rate > 2% over last 60 min → insert `monitoring_alerts` row + log WARNING
- Job late by > 10 min → insert alert + log WARNING
- Alerts surfaced via `GET /api/monitoring/alerts`

### Grafana
- Pre-provisioned dashboard JSON in `monitoring/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/`
- Datasource: Prometheus at `http://prometheus:9090`

## 15. Dockerization Plan

### Services in `docker-compose.yml`

| Service | Image | Port |
|---|---|---|
| `backend` | ./backend/Dockerfile | 8000 |
| `nginx` | ./nginx/Dockerfile | 80 |
| `db` | postgres:15-alpine | 5432 |
| `redis` | redis:7-alpine | 6379 |
| `celery_worker` | same as backend | — |
| `celery_beat` | same as backend | — |
| `kafka` | confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.6.0 | 9092 |
| `zookeeper` | confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.6.0 | 2181 |
| `prometheus` | prom/prometheus:latest | 9090 |
| `grafana` | grafana/grafana:latest | 3000 |

### Backend Dockerfile
```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./
RUN pip install poetry && poetry install --no-root --no-dev
COPY . .
RUN poetry install --no-dev
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
```

### Startup Sequence (depends_on + healthchecks)
```
zookeeper → kafka
db (healthcheck: pg_isready) → backend → celery_worker, celery_beat
redis → backend → celery_worker, celery_beat
backend → nginx
prometheus → grafana
```

### Entrypoint Script (`docker-entrypoint.sh`)
1. Wait for DB healthcheck
2. Run `alembic upgrade head`
3. Seed default admin user if `users` table is empty
4. Start uvicorn

### Volumes
```yaml
volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:
  exports_data:         # reconciliation + search exports
  grafana_data:
  prometheus_data:
```

## 16. Database Migrations Strategy

- `alembic/env.py` imports all SQLAlchemy models for autogenerate
- Each migration: forward + downgrade
- Initial migration: all tables + indexes + extensions (pg_trgm, uuid-ossp)
- Subsequent migrations: additive only (no column drops without transition period)
- Run in entrypoint before app start

---

## 17. Testing Strategy

### Structure
```
backend/tests/
├── conftest.py         # async test DB, fixture factories, test client
├── unit/
│   ├── test_security.py
│   ├── test_best_offer.py
│   ├── test_booking_state.py
│   └── test_encryption.py
└── api/
    ├── test_auth.py
    ├── test_sessions.py
    ├── test_bookings.py
    ├── test_checkout.py
    ├── test_payments.py
    ├── test_search.py
    └── test_ingestion.py
```

### Approach
- **Unit tests**: pure logic (best-offer engine, booking rule checks, password validator, Fernet encrypt/decrypt)
- **API tests**: `httpx.AsyncClient` against test app instance with a real PostgreSQL test DB (separate DB, not mocked)
- **Fixtures**: `pytest-asyncio`, factory functions for users/sessions/bookings/orders
- **Test DB**: separate `meridian_test` database; `conftest.py` runs `alembic upgrade head` once, wraps each test in a transaction rollback
- **Coverage target**: 80%+ on service + controller layers

## Context

This plan covers the full backend implementation for the Meridian Training Operations System: a production-grade, fully offline-capable training management platform. The backend serves five user roles (Administrator, Instructor, Learner, Finance Analyst, Data Ops) and orchestrates class scheduling, live/recorded session access, booking flows, offline checkout with promotions, staff search, payments, background jobs, and multi-source data ingestion — all running in a single `docker compose up` environment.

---

## 1. Tech Stack Summary

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | PyNest (wraps FastAPI) — Python 3.11+ |
| Database | PostgreSQL 15 |
| ORM | SQLAlchemy 2.x (async) + asyncpg |
| Migrations | Alembic |
| Auth/Crypto | Passlib (bcrypt), cryptography (Fernet) |
| Cache/Broker | Redis 7 |
| Background Jobs | Celery 5 + Celery Beat |
| Streaming Ingest | Kafka (confluent-kafka-python) |
| Monitoring | Prometheus + Grafana |
| Search | PostgreSQL Full-Text Search + pg_trgm |
| Testing | pytest + httpx (async) |
| Reverse Proxy | Nginx |
| Containerization | Docker + Docker Compose |

Each `modules/<name>/` follows PyNest module conventions:
```
<name>/

## 3. Database Schema & Entity Relationships

### Core Entities

**users**
- id (UUID PK), username (unique), password_hash, role (enum: admin/instructor/learner/finance/dataops)
- email_encrypted, phone_encrypted (Fernet at rest)
- is_active, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, last_login
- created_at, updated_at

**locations**
- id, name, address, timezone (IANA string), is_active

**rooms**
- id, location_id (FK), name, capacity, is_active

**courses**
- id, title, description, duration_minutes, category, is_active

**instructors**
- id, user_id (FK unique), bio, is_active

**sessions**
- id, course_id (FK), instructor_id (FK), room_id (FK)
- start_time (timestamptz), end_time (timestamptz)
- capacity, enrolled_count, buffer_minutes (default 10)
- status (enum: scheduled/live/completed/cancelled)
- recurrence_rule_id (FK nullable) — RRULE reference
- created_by (FK users), created_at, updated_at

**recurrence_rules**
- id, rrule_string (e.g., "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=TU,TH;BYHOUR=18;BYMINUTE=30")
- start_date, end_date, parent_session_id (FK)

**bookings**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- status (enum: pending/confirmed/rescheduled/cancelled/no_show)
- rescheduled_from_id (FK self nullable)
- policy_fee_flagged (bool), cancellation_reason
- confirmed_at, cancelled_at, created_at, updated_at

**attendance_records**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- joined_at, left_at, minutes_attended, was_late (bool)

**replay_access_rules**
- id, session_id (FK), rule_type (enrolled_only/public/custom)
- available_from (timestamptz), available_until (timestamptz)

**replay_views**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK), watched_seconds, completed (bool), viewed_at

**promotions**
- id, name, type (enum: pct_off/fixed_off/bogo), value (numeric)
- min_order_amount (nullable), applies_to (course_ids jsonb, or "all")
- stack_group (nullable), is_exclusive (bool), is_active
- valid_from, valid_until

**orders**
- id, learner_id (FK), status (enum: pending/paid/cancelled/refunded)
- subtotal, discount_total, total, currency
- created_at, paid_at, closed_at, expires_at (30-min auto-close)

**order_items**
- id, order_id (FK), session_id (FK), unit_price, quantity

**order_promotions** (applied promos per order)
- id, order_id (FK), promotion_id (FK), discount_amount, explanation (text)

**payments**
- id, order_id (FK), terminal_ref, amount, status (enum: pending/completed/failed)
- callback_received_at, signature_verified (bool), raw_callback (jsonb)

**refunds**
- id, payment_id (FK), requested_by (FK users), amount, reason
- status (enum: pending/approved/completed/rejected)
- created_at, processed_at

**reconciliation_exports**
- id, export_date (date), file_path, generated_at, row_count

**saved_searches**
- id, user_id (FK), name, filters_json, created_at, updated_at
- (max 20 per user — enforced at service layer)

**audit_logs**
- id, actor_id (FK users), entity_type (varchar), entity_id (uuid)
- action (varchar), old_value (jsonb), new_value (jsonb)
- ip_address, created_at
- (90-day retention enforced by Celery purge job)

**ingestion_sources**
- id, name, type (enum: kafka/logstash/batch_file/cdc_mysql/cdc_pg)
- config_encrypted (Fernet jsonb), collection_frequency_seconds
- concurrency_cap, is_active, last_run_at, last_status

**ingestion_runs**
- id, source_id (FK), started_at, finished_at, rows_ingested, status, error_detail

**job_executions** (scheduler monitoring)
- id, job_name, started_at, finished_at, status (success/failure/late), error_detail

### Key Indexes

```sql
-- Sessions calendar queries
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_start_time ON sessions(start_time);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_instructor ON sessions(instructor_id);

-- Booking lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_learner ON bookings(learner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_session ON bookings(session_id);

-- Full-text search on users
CREATE INDEX idx_users_fts ON users USING GIN(to_tsvector('english', username));

-- pg_trgm for fuzzy search
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
CREATE INDEX idx_users_phone_trgm ON users USING GIN(phone_encrypted gin_trgm_ops); -- on decrypted view
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_id_trgm ON orders USING GIN(CAST(id AS text) gin_trgm_ops);

-- Audit log retention query
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_created ON audit_logs(created_at);

-- Promotions validity
CREATE INDEX idx_promotions_valid ON promotions(valid_from, valid_until) WHERE is_active = TRUE;
```

## 4. Authentication & Authorization

### Auth Flow
- `POST /api/auth/login` — validate username + password (bcrypt verify), check lockout, return JWT (access + refresh)
- `POST /api/auth/refresh` — exchange refresh token
- `POST /api/auth/logout` — invalidate refresh token (Redis blocklist)
- Session inactivity: access token TTL = 30 minutes; refresh extends on activity
- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts → locked for 15 minutes (stored on `users.locked_until`)

### Password Policy (enforced in Pydantic validator)
- Minimum 12 characters
- At least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, one special character
- Checked via regex in `core/security.py`

### JWT
- Algorithm: HS256 (secret from env var, never hardcoded)
- Access token payload: `sub` (user_id), `role`, `exp`, `iat`
- Refresh tokens stored as hashed value in Redis with TTL

### Role-Based Access Control
- FastAPI dependency `require_role(*roles)` applied per router
- Role matrix:

| Endpoint Group | admin | instructor | learner | finance | dataops |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Locations/Rooms CRUD | ✓ | | | | |
| Sessions CRUD | ✓ | own only | | | |
| Bookings | ✓ | read | own only | read | |
| Checkout/Orders | ✓ | | own only | ✓ | |
| Payments/Refunds | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Promotions CRUD | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Search & Export | ✓ | ✓ | | ✓ | |
| Ingestion Config | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Job Monitoring | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Audit Logs | ✓ | | | | |

## 6. Booking State Machine

```
PENDING ──────────────────────────────► CONFIRMED
    │                                       │
    │                              ┌────────┴────────┐
    │                              ▼                 ▼
    │                        RESCHEDULED         CANCELLED
    │                              │                 │
    └──────────────────────────────►      (terminal: NO_SHOW from CONFIRMED)
```

**Rules enforced in `bookings/service.py`:**
- Reschedule blocked if `session.start_time - now < 2 hours`
- Cancellation within 24 hours → `policy_fee_flagged = True`
- Cancellation decrements `sessions.enrolled_count`
- All state transitions appended to `audit_logs`

---

## 7. Best-Offer Checkout Engine (`checkout/service.py`)

```
Input: order_items (session_ids + quantities)
Step 1: Load all active promotions valid now for the order's courses
Step 2: Group by stack_group; is_exclusive promos form their own group
Step 3: For each valid combination:
   - Apply eligible promos
   - Compute total discount
Step 4: Select combination with maximum discount (tie-break: fewest promos)
Step 5: Build explanation list:
   [{"promo": "10% off", "amount": 15.00, "reason": "10% discount applied to Workshop A"}]
Step 6: Persist order_promotions rows with explanation text
```

Stack controls:
- `is_exclusive=True` → that promo cannot combine with any other
- Same `stack_group` → mutually exclusive (only one from group applied)
- Different groups / null group → stackable

---

## 8. Payment & Reconciliation

### Offline Payment Ledger Flow
1. Order confirmed → `payments` row created (status=pending), order `expires_at = now + 30min`
2. Celery Beat task every 1 min: close unpaid orders past `expires_at`
3. LAN terminal POSTs callback to `/api/payments/callback`
4. Signature verification: `HMAC-SHA256(secret_key, terminal_ref + amount + timestamp)` — reject if invalid
5. Payment status → completed, order status → paid, `paid_at` set

### Refund Flow
Finance analyst → `POST /api/refunds` → status=pending → admin/finance approve → status=completed → payment ledger updated

### Daily Reconciliation Export
- Celery Beat: `0 2 * * *` → `jobs/tasks.py::generate_reconciliation_export`
- Query: all completed payments for previous calendar day
- Write CSV to `EXPORTS_DIR/reconciliation_YYYY-MM-DD.csv`
- Insert row in `reconciliation_exports`

---

## 9. Background Jobs (Celery + Beat)

All tasks defined in `modules/jobs/tasks.py`:

| Task | Schedule | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `attendance_rollup` | Every 5 min | Aggregate session attendance stats |
| `close_expired_orders` | Every 1 min | Auto-close unpaid orders past 30 min |
| `generate_reconciliation_export` | 2:00 AM daily | Finance CSV export |
| `purge_audit_logs` | 3:00 AM daily | Delete audit_logs older than 90 days |
| `run_ingestion_jobs` | Configurable per source | Trigger enabled ingestion sources |
| `check_job_health` | Every 2 min | Alert if failure rate > 2% in last hour or job late > 10 min |
| `replay_access_expiry` | Every 10 min | Mark expired replay rules as inactive |

**Retry policy:** `max_retries=3`, `retry_backoff=True`, `retry_backoff_max=300` (5 min cap)

**Monitoring:** Each task wraps execution in try/finally, writes to `job_executions`, increments Prometheus counters (`job_success_total`, `job_failure_total`, `job_duration_seconds`).

## 10. Data Ingestion Pipeline

### Source Types (`modules/ingestion/`)

**Kafka** (`ingestion/adapters/kafka_adapter.py`)
- `confluent_kafka.Consumer` subscribing to configured topics
- Batch consume up to `concurrency_cap` messages per cycle
- Deserialize (JSON/Avro), validate against source schema, upsert via deterministic key

**Logstash / Flume (HTTP push)**
- Ingestion endpoint: `POST /api/ingestion/webhook/{source_id}` authenticated by source API key
- Validate, queue to Celery for async processing

**Batch File Import**
- Watched directory (Docker volume mount) or upload endpoint
- CSV/JSON parsing, schema validation with row-level error logging
- Deterministic reprocessing via file hash + row fingerprint (skip duplicates)

**CDC — MySQL/PostgreSQL**
- For MySQL: `mysql-connector-python` + binlog position tracking
- For PostgreSQL: logical replication slot via `psycopg2`
- Row-level upserts with `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`

### Connectivity Check
`POST /api/ingestion/sources/{id}/test-connection` → attempt connection, return latency + error if any

### Duplicate Prevention
Each source row hashed (source_id + natural key) → stored in `ingestion_dedup` Redis set with TTL = 7 days

## 11. Search Design

### Advanced Search (`POST /api/search`)

Filters accepted:
```json
{
  "invoice_number": "string",
  "learner_phone": "string",       // matched against masked/decrypted
  "enrollment_status": "confirmed",
  "date_range": {"from": "...", "to": "..."},
  "site_id": "uuid",
  "instructor_id": "uuid",
  "page": 1,
  "page_size": 50
}
```

Implementation:
- Base query on `bookings` joined with `sessions`, `users`, `locations`, `orders`
- Phone search: decrypt + substring match OR pg_trgm similarity
- Invoice number: trgm index on `CAST(orders.id AS text)`
- FTS fallback for keyword fields: `to_tsvector || plainto_tsquery`
- Pagination: keyset pagination (cursor on `created_at + id`) for performance
- "Results updated" feedback: response includes `total_count` + `query_time_ms`

### CSV/Excel Export
- `POST /api/search/export` runs same query, streams results in batches of 1000
- Hard cap: 50,000 rows → HTTP 400 if count exceeds
- CSV via Python `csv` module; Excel via `openpyxl`
- Returned as streaming response with `Content-Disposition` header

## 12. Sensitive Data Handling

### Field Encryption
`core/encryption.py` — Fernet symmetric encryption:
- Key loaded from env `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY`
- `encrypt_field(value: str) -> str` / `decrypt_field(value: str) -> str`
- Applied to: `users.email`, `users.phone`, `ingestion_sources.config`

### Masking in API Responses
- `users/schemas.py` `UserResponse` schema: phone shown as `+1-***-***-1234`
- Masking logic in `shared/utils/masking.py`
- Admin role: `UserDetailResponse` includes unmasked values

### Audit Logging
`core/audit.py::log_audit(actor_id, entity_type, entity_id, action, old_value, new_value, request)`  
Triggered on: session schedule changes, promotion changes, refund actions, access control changes

## 13. Monitoring & Alerting

### Prometheus Metrics (exposed at `/api/monitoring/metrics`)
- `http_request_duration_seconds` (histogram, by route + status)
- `job_success_total` / `job_failure_total` (counter, by job_name)
- `job_duration_seconds` (histogram)
- `active_sessions_gauge`
- `pending_orders_gauge`
- `ingestion_rows_total` (counter, by source)

### Alert Rules (evaluated in `jobs/tasks.py::check_job_health`)
- Failure rate > 2% over last 60 min → insert `monitoring_alerts` row + log WARNING
- Job late by > 10 min → insert alert + log WARNING
- Alerts surfaced via `GET /api/monitoring/alerts`

### Grafana
- Pre-provisioned dashboard JSON in `monitoring/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/`
- Datasource: Prometheus at `http://prometheus:9090`

## 15. Dockerization Plan

### Services in `docker-compose.yml`

| Service | Image | Port |
|---|---|---|
| `backend` | ./backend/Dockerfile | 8000 |
| `nginx` | ./nginx/Dockerfile | 80 |
| `db` | postgres:15-alpine | 5432 |
| `redis` | redis:7-alpine | 6379 |
| `celery_worker` | same as backend | — |
| `celery_beat` | same as backend | — |
| `kafka` | confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.6.0 | 9092 |
| `zookeeper` | confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.6.0 | 2181 |
| `prometheus` | prom/prometheus:latest | 9090 |
| `grafana` | grafana/grafana:latest | 3000 |

### Backend Dockerfile
```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./
RUN pip install poetry && poetry install --no-root --no-dev
COPY . .
RUN poetry install --no-dev
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
```

### Startup Sequence (depends_on + healthchecks)
```
zookeeper → kafka
db (healthcheck: pg_isready) → backend → celery_worker, celery_beat
redis → backend → celery_worker, celery_beat
backend → nginx
prometheus → grafana
```

### Entrypoint Script (`docker-entrypoint.sh`)
1. Wait for DB healthcheck
2. Run `alembic upgrade head`
3. Seed default admin user if `users` table is empty
4. Start uvicorn

### Volumes
```yaml
volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:
  exports_data:         # reconciliation + search exports
  grafana_data:
  prometheus_data:
```

## 16. Database Migrations Strategy

- `alembic/env.py` imports all SQLAlchemy models for autogenerate
- Each migration: forward + downgrade
- Initial migration: all tables + indexes + extensions (pg_trgm, uuid-ossp)
- Subsequent migrations: additive only (no column drops without transition period)
- Run in entrypoint before app start

## 17. Testing Strategy

### Structure
```
backend/tests/
├── conftest.py         # async test DB, fixture factories, test client
├── unit/
│   ├── test_security.py
│   ├── test_best_offer.py
│   ├── test_booking_state.py
│   └── test_encryption.py
└── api/
    ├── test_auth.py
    ├── test_sessions.py
    ├── test_bookings.py
    ├── test_checkout.py
    ├── test_payments.py
    ├── test_search.py
    └── test_ingestion.py
```

### Approach
- **Unit tests**: pure logic (best-offer engine, booking rule checks, password validator, Fernet encrypt/decrypt)
- **API tests**: `httpx.AsyncClient` against test app instance with a real PostgreSQL test DB (separate DB, not mocked)
- **Fixtures**: `pytest-asyncio`, factory functions for users/sessions/bookings/orders
- **Test DB**: separate `meridian_test` database; `conftest.py` runs `alembic upgrade head` once, wraps each test in a transaction rollback
- **Coverage target**: 80%+ on service + controller layers

This plan covers the full backend implementation for the Meridian Training Operations System: a production-grade, fully offline-capable training management platform. The backend serves five user roles (Administrator, Instructor, Learner, Finance Analyst, Data Ops) and orchestrates class scheduling, live/recorded session access, booking flows, offline checkout with promotions, staff search, payments, background jobs, and multi-source data ingestion — all running in a single `docker compose up` environment.

## 3. Database Schema & Entity Relationships

### Core Entities

**users**
- id (UUID PK), username (unique), password_hash, role (enum: admin/instructor/learner/finance/dataops)
- email_encrypted, phone_encrypted (Fernet at rest)
- is_active, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, last_login
- created_at, updated_at

**locations**
- id, name, address, timezone (IANA string), is_active

**rooms**
- id, location_id (FK), name, capacity, is_active

**courses**
- id, title, description, duration_minutes, category, is_active

**instructors**
- id, user_id (FK unique), bio, is_active

**sessions**
- id, course_id (FK), instructor_id (FK), room_id (FK)
- start_time (timestamptz), end_time (timestamptz)
- capacity, enrolled_count, buffer_minutes (default 10)
- status (enum: scheduled/live/completed/cancelled)
- recurrence_rule_id (FK nullable) — RRULE reference
- created_by (FK users), created_at, updated_at

**recurrence_rules**
- id, rrule_string (e.g., "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=TU,TH;BYHOUR=18;BYMINUTE=30")
- start_date, end_date, parent_session_id (FK)

**bookings**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- status (enum: pending/confirmed/rescheduled/cancelled/no_show)
- rescheduled_from_id (FK self nullable)
- policy_fee_flagged (bool), cancellation_reason
- confirmed_at, cancelled_at, created_at, updated_at

**attendance_records**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- joined_at, left_at, minutes_attended, was_late (bool)

**replay_access_rules**
- id, session_id (FK), rule_type (enrolled_only/public/custom)
- available_from (timestamptz), available_until (timestamptz)

**replay_views**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK), watched_seconds, completed (bool), viewed_at

**promotions**
- id, name, type (enum: pct_off/fixed_off/bogo), value (numeric)
- min_order_amount (nullable), applies_to (course_ids jsonb, or "all")
- stack_group (nullable), is_exclusive (bool), is_active
- valid_from, valid_until

**orders**
- id, learner_id (FK), status (enum: pending/paid/cancelled/refunded)
- subtotal, discount_total, total, currency
- created_at, paid_at, closed_at, expires_at (30-min auto-close)

**order_items**
- id, order_id (FK), session_id (FK), unit_price, quantity

**order_promotions** (applied promos per order)
- id, order_id (FK), promotion_id (FK), discount_amount, explanation (text)

**payments**
- id, order_id (FK), terminal_ref, amount, status (enum: pending/completed/failed)
- callback_received_at, signature_verified (bool), raw_callback (jsonb)

**refunds**
- id, payment_id (FK), requested_by (FK users), amount, reason
- status (enum: pending/approved/completed/rejected)
- created_at, processed_at

**reconciliation_exports**
- id, export_date (date), file_path, generated_at, row_count

**saved_searches**
- id, user_id (FK), name, filters_json, created_at, updated_at
- (max 20 per user — enforced at service layer)

**audit_logs**
- id, actor_id (FK users), entity_type (varchar), entity_id (uuid)
- action (varchar), old_value (jsonb), new_value (jsonb)
- ip_address, created_at
- (90-day retention enforced by Celery purge job)

**ingestion_sources**
- id, name, type (enum: kafka/logstash/batch_file/cdc_mysql/cdc_pg)
- config_encrypted (Fernet jsonb), collection_frequency_seconds
- concurrency_cap, is_active, last_run_at, last_status

**ingestion_runs**
- id, source_id (FK), started_at, finished_at, rows_ingested, status, error_detail

**job_executions** (scheduler monitoring)
- id, job_name, started_at, finished_at, status (success/failure/late), error_detail

### Key Indexes

```sql
-- Sessions calendar queries
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_start_time ON sessions(start_time);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_instructor ON sessions(instructor_id);

-- Booking lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_learner ON bookings(learner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_session ON bookings(session_id);

-- Full-text search on users
CREATE INDEX idx_users_fts ON users USING GIN(to_tsvector('english', username));

-- pg_trgm for fuzzy search
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
CREATE INDEX idx_users_phone_trgm ON users USING GIN(phone_encrypted gin_trgm_ops); -- on decrypted view
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_id_trgm ON orders USING GIN(CAST(id AS text) gin_trgm_ops);

-- Audit log retention query
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_created ON audit_logs(created_at);

-- Promotions validity
CREATE INDEX idx_promotions_valid ON promotions(valid_from, valid_until) WHERE is_active = TRUE;
```

## 4. Authentication & Authorization

### Auth Flow
- `POST /api/auth/login` — validate username + password (bcrypt verify), check lockout, return JWT (access + refresh)
- `POST /api/auth/refresh` — exchange refresh token
- `POST /api/auth/logout` — invalidate refresh token (Redis blocklist)
- Session inactivity: access token TTL = 30 minutes; refresh extends on activity
- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts → locked for 15 minutes (stored on `users.locked_until`)

### Password Policy (enforced in Pydantic validator)
- Minimum 12 characters
- At least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, one special character
- Checked via regex in `core/security.py`

### JWT
- Algorithm: HS256 (secret from env var, never hardcoded)
- Access token payload: `sub` (user_id), `role`, `exp`, `iat`
- Refresh tokens stored as hashed value in Redis with TTL

### Role-Based Access Control
- FastAPI dependency `require_role(*roles)` applied per router
- Role matrix:

| Endpoint Group | admin | instructor | learner | finance | dataops |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Locations/Rooms CRUD | ✓ | | | | |
| Sessions CRUD | ✓ | own only | | | |
| Bookings | ✓ | read | own only | read | |
| Checkout/Orders | ✓ | | own only | ✓ | |
| Payments/Refunds | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Promotions CRUD | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Search & Export | ✓ | ✓ | | ✓ | |
| Ingestion Config | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Job Monitoring | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Audit Logs | ✓ | | | | |

## 6. Booking State Machine

```
PENDING ──────────────────────────────► CONFIRMED
    │                                       │
    │                              ┌────────┴────────┐
    │                              ▼                 ▼
    │                        RESCHEDULED         CANCELLED
    │                              │                 │
    └──────────────────────────────►      (terminal: NO_SHOW from CONFIRMED)
```

**Rules enforced in `bookings/service.py`:**
- Reschedule blocked if `session.start_time - now < 2 hours`
- Cancellation within 24 hours → `policy_fee_flagged = True`
- Cancellation decrements `sessions.enrolled_count`
- All state transitions appended to `audit_logs`

---

## 7. Best-Offer Checkout Engine (`checkout/service.py`)

```
Input: order_items (session_ids + quantities)
Step 1: Load all active promotions valid now for the order's courses
Step 2: Group by stack_group; is_exclusive promos form their own group
Step 3: For each valid combination:
   - Apply eligible promos
   - Compute total discount
Step 4: Select combination with maximum discount (tie-break: fewest promos)
Step 5: Build explanation list:
   [{"promo": "10% off", "amount": 15.00, "reason": "10% discount applied to Workshop A"}]
Step 6: Persist order_promotions rows with explanation text
```

Stack controls:
- `is_exclusive=True` → that promo cannot combine with any other
- Same `stack_group` → mutually exclusive (only one from group applied)
- Different groups / null group → stackable

---

## 8. Payment & Reconciliation

### Offline Payment Ledger Flow
1. Order confirmed → `payments` row created (status=pending), order `expires_at = now + 30min`
2. Celery Beat task every 1 min: close unpaid orders past `expires_at`
3. LAN terminal POSTs callback to `/api/payments/callback`
4. Signature verification: `HMAC-SHA256(secret_key, terminal_ref + amount + timestamp)` — reject if invalid
5. Payment status → completed, order status → paid, `paid_at` set

### Refund Flow
Finance analyst → `POST /api/refunds` → status=pending → admin/finance approve → status=completed → payment ledger updated

### Daily Reconciliation Export
- Celery Beat: `0 2 * * *` → `jobs/tasks.py::generate_reconciliation_export`
- Query: all completed payments for previous calendar day
- Write CSV to `EXPORTS_DIR/reconciliation_YYYY-MM-DD.csv`
- Insert row in `reconciliation_exports`

---

## 9. Background Jobs (Celery + Beat)

All tasks defined in `modules/jobs/tasks.py`:

| Task | Schedule | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `attendance_rollup` | Every 5 min | Aggregate session attendance stats |
| `close_expired_orders` | Every 1 min | Auto-close unpaid orders past 30 min |
| `generate_reconciliation_export` | 2:00 AM daily | Finance CSV export |
| `purge_audit_logs` | 3:00 AM daily | Delete audit_logs older than 90 days |
| `run_ingestion_jobs` | Configurable per source | Trigger enabled ingestion sources |
| `check_job_health` | Every 2 min | Alert if failure rate > 2% in last hour or job late > 10 min |
| `replay_access_expiry` | Every 10 min | Mark expired replay rules as inactive |

**Retry policy:** `max_retries=3`, `retry_backoff=True`, `retry_backoff_max=300` (5 min cap)

**Monitoring:** Each task wraps execution in try/finally, writes to `job_executions`, increments Prometheus counters (`job_success_total`, `job_failure_total`, `job_duration_seconds`).

---

## 10. Data Ingestion Pipeline

### Source Types (`modules/ingestion/`)

**Kafka** (`ingestion/adapters/kafka_adapter.py`)
- `confluent_kafka.Consumer` subscribing to configured topics
- Batch consume up to `concurrency_cap` messages per cycle
- Deserialize (JSON/Avro), validate against source schema, upsert via deterministic key

**Logstash / Flume (HTTP push)**
- Ingestion endpoint: `POST /api/ingestion/webhook/{source_id}` authenticated by source API key
- Validate, queue to Celery for async processing

**Batch File Import**
- Watched directory (Docker volume mount) or upload endpoint
- CSV/JSON parsing, schema validation with row-level error logging
- Deterministic reprocessing via file hash + row fingerprint (skip duplicates)

**CDC — MySQL/PostgreSQL**
- For MySQL: `mysql-connector-python` + binlog position tracking
- For PostgreSQL: logical replication slot via `psycopg2`
- Row-level upserts with `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`

### Connectivity Check
`POST /api/ingestion/sources/{id}/test-connection` → attempt connection, return latency + error if any

### Duplicate Prevention
Each source row hashed (source_id + natural key) → stored in `ingestion_dedup` Redis set with TTL = 7 days

---

## 11. Search Design

### Advanced Search (`POST /api/search`)

Filters accepted:
```json
{
  "invoice_number": "string",
  "learner_phone": "string",       // matched against masked/decrypted
  "enrollment_status": "confirmed",
  "date_range": {"from": "...", "to": "..."},
  "site_id": "uuid",
  "instructor_id": "uuid",
  "page": 1,
  "page_size": 50
}
```

Implementation:
- Base query on `bookings` joined with `sessions`, `users`, `locations`, `orders`
- Phone search: decrypt + substring match OR pg_trgm similarity
- Invoice number: trgm index on `CAST(orders.id AS text)`
- FTS fallback for keyword fields: `to_tsvector || plainto_tsquery`
- Pagination: keyset pagination (cursor on `created_at + id`) for performance
- "Results updated" feedback: response includes `total_count` + `query_time_ms`

### CSV/Excel Export
- `POST /api/search/export` runs same query, streams results in batches of 1000
- Hard cap: 50,000 rows → HTTP 400 if count exceeds
- CSV via Python `csv` module; Excel via `openpyxl`
- Returned as streaming response with `Content-Disposition` header

## 12. Sensitive Data Handling

### Field Encryption
`core/encryption.py` — Fernet symmetric encryption:
- Key loaded from env `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY`
- `encrypt_field(value: str) -> str` / `decrypt_field(value: str) -> str`
- Applied to: `users.email`, `users.phone`, `ingestion_sources.config`

### Masking in API Responses
- `users/schemas.py` `UserResponse` schema: phone shown as `+1-***-***-1234`
- Masking logic in `shared/utils/masking.py`
- Admin role: `UserDetailResponse` includes unmasked values

### Audit Logging
`core/audit.py::log_audit(actor_id, entity_type, entity_id, action, old_value, new_value, request)`  
Triggered on: session schedule changes, promotion changes, refund actions, access control changes

- `http_request_duration_seconds` (histogram, by route + status)

- `job_duration_seconds` (histogram)

### Backend Dockerfile
```dockerfile
FROM python:3.11-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./
RUN pip install poetry && poetry install --no-root --no-dev
COPY . .
RUN poetry install --no-dev
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
```

### Startup Sequence (depends_on + healthchecks)
```
zookeeper → kafka
db (healthcheck: pg_isready) → backend → celery_worker, celery_beat
redis → backend → celery_worker, celery_beat
backend → nginx
prometheus → grafana
```

### Entrypoint Script (`docker-entrypoint.sh`)
1. Wait for DB healthcheck
2. Run `alembic upgrade head`
3. Seed default admin user if `users` table is empty
4. Start uvicorn

### Volumes
```yaml
volumes:
  postgres_data:
  redis_data:
  exports_data:         # reconciliation + search exports
  grafana_data:
  prometheus_data:
```

## 16. Database Migrations Strategy

- `alembic/env.py` imports all SQLAlchemy models for autogenerate
- Each migration: forward + downgrade
- Initial migration: all tables + indexes + extensions (pg_trgm, uuid-ossp)
- Subsequent migrations: additive only (no column drops without transition period)
- Run in entrypoint before app start

---

├── conftest.py         # async test DB, fixture factories, test client
├── unit/

```

### Approach
- **Unit tests**: pure logic (best-offer engine, booking rule checks, password validator, Fernet encrypt/decrypt)
- **API tests**: `httpx.AsyncClient` against test app instance with a real PostgreSQL test DB (separate DB, not mocked)
- **Fixtures**: `pytest-asyncio`, factory functions for users/sessions/bookings/orders
- **Test DB**: separate `meridian_test` database; `conftest.py` runs `alembic upgrade head` once, wraps each test in a transaction rollback
- **Coverage target**: 80%+ on service + controller layers

## Approved Plan:
# Meridian Training Operations System — Backend Blueprint

## Context

This plan covers the full backend implementation for the Meridian Training Operations System: a production-grade, fully offline-capable training management platform. The backend serves five user roles (Administrator, Instructor, Learner, Finance Analyst, Data Ops) and orchestrates class scheduling, live/recorded session access, booking flows, offline checkout with promotions, staff search, payments, background jobs, and multi-source data ingestion — all running in a single `docker compose up` environment.

---

## 1. Tech Stack Summary

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | PyNest (wraps FastAPI) — Python 3.11+ |
| Database | PostgreSQL 15 |
| ORM | SQLAlchemy 2.x (async) + asyncpg |
| Migrations | Alembic |
| Auth/Crypto | Passlib (bcrypt), cryptography (Fernet) |
| Cache/Broker | Redis 7 |
| Background Jobs | Celery 5 + Celery Beat |
| Streaming Ingest | Kafka (confluent-kafka-python) |
| Monitoring | Prometheus + Grafana |
| Search | PostgreSQL Full-Text Search + pg_trgm |
| Testing | pytest + httpx (async) |
| Reverse Proxy | Nginx |
| Containerization | Docker + Docker Compose |

---

## 2. Repository Structure

```
repo/

│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── pyproject.toml           # dependencies (Poetry)
│   ├── alembic/

## 3. Database Schema & Entity Relationships

### Core Entities

**users**
- id (UUID PK), username (unique), password_hash, role (enum: admin/instructor/learner/finance/dataops)
- email_encrypted, phone_encrypted (Fernet at rest)
- is_active, failed_login_attempts, locked_until, last_login
- created_at, updated_at

**locations**
- id, name, address, timezone (IANA string), is_active

**rooms**
- id, location_id (FK), name, capacity, is_active

**courses**
- id, title, description, duration_minutes, category, is_active

**instructors**
- id, user_id (FK unique), bio, is_active

**sessions**
- id, course_id (FK), instructor_id (FK), room_id (FK)
- start_time (timestamptz), end_time (timestamptz)
- capacity, enrolled_count, buffer_minutes (default 10)
- status (enum: scheduled/live/completed/cancelled)
- recurrence_rule_id (FK nullable) — RRULE reference
- created_by (FK users), created_at, updated_at

**recurrence_rules**
- id, rrule_string (e.g., "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=TU,TH;BYHOUR=18;BYMINUTE=30")
- start_date, end_date, parent_session_id (FK)

**bookings**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- status (enum: pending/confirmed/rescheduled/cancelled/no_show)
- rescheduled_from_id (FK self nullable)
- policy_fee_flagged (bool), cancellation_reason
- confirmed_at, cancelled_at, created_at, updated_at

**attendance_records**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK users)
- joined_at, left_at, minutes_attended, was_late (bool)

**replay_access_rules**
- id, session_id (FK), rule_type (enrolled_only/public/custom)
- available_from (timestamptz), available_until (timestamptz)

**replay_views**
- id, session_id (FK), learner_id (FK), watched_seconds, completed (bool), viewed_at

**promotions**
- id, name, type (enum: pct_off/fixed_off/bogo), value (numeric)
- min_order_amount (nullable), applies_to (course_ids jsonb, or "all")
- stack_group (nullable), is_exclusive (bool), is_active
- valid_from, valid_until

**orders**
- id, learner_id (FK), status (enum: pending/paid/cancelled/refunded)
- subtotal, discount_total, total, currency
- created_at, paid_at, closed_at, expires_at (30-min auto-close)

**order_items**
- id, order_id (FK), session_id (FK), unit_price, quantity

**order_promotions** (applied promos per order)
- id, order_id (FK), promotion_id (FK), discount_amount, explanation (text)

**payments**
- id, order_id (FK), terminal_ref, amount, status (enum: pending/completed/failed)
- callback_received_at, signature_verified (bool), raw_callback (jsonb)

**refunds**
- id, payment_id (FK), requested_by (FK users), amount, reason
- status (enum: pending/approved/completed/rejected)
- created_at, processed_at

**reconciliation_exports**
- id, export_date (date), file_path, generated_at, row_count

**saved_searches**
- id, user_id (FK), name, filters_json, created_at, updated_at
- (max 20 per user — enforced at service layer)

**audit_logs**
- id, actor_id (FK users), entity_type (varchar), entity_id (uuid)
- action (varchar), old_value (jsonb), new_value (jsonb)
- ip_address, created_at
- (90-day retention enforced by Celery purge job)

**ingestion_sources**
- id, name, type (enum: kafka/logstash/batch_file/cdc_mysql/cdc_pg)
- config_encrypted (Fernet jsonb), collection_frequency_seconds
- concurrency_cap, is_active, last_run_at, last_status

**ingestion_runs**
- id, source_id (FK), started_at, finished_at, rows_ingested, status, error_detail

**job_executions** (scheduler monitoring)
- id, job_name, started_at, finished_at, status (success/failure/late), error_detail

### Key Indexes

```sql
-- Sessions calendar queries
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_start_time ON sessions(start_time);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_instructor ON sessions(instructor_id);

-- Booking lookups
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_learner ON bookings(learner_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_bookings_session ON bookings(session_id);

-- Full-text search on users
CREATE INDEX idx_users_fts ON users USING GIN(to_tsvector('english', username));

-- pg_trgm for fuzzy search
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
CREATE INDEX idx_users_phone_trgm ON users USING GIN(phone_encrypted gin_trgm_ops); -- on decrypted view
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_id_trgm ON orders USING GIN(CAST(id AS text) gin_trgm_ops);

-- Audit log retention query
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_created ON audit_logs(created_at);

-- Promotions validity
CREATE INDEX idx_promotions_valid ON promotions(valid_from, valid_until) WHERE is_active = TRUE;
```

## 4. Authentication & Authorization

### Auth Flow
- `POST /api/auth/login` — validate username + password (bcrypt verify), check lockout, return JWT (access + refresh)
- `POST /api/auth/refresh` — exchange refresh token
- `POST /api/auth/logout` — invalidate refresh token (Redis blocklist)
- Session inactivity: access token TTL = 30 minutes; refresh extends on activity
- Account lockout: 5 failed attempts → locked for 15 minutes (stored on `users.locked_until`)

### Password Policy (enforced in Pydantic validator)
- Minimum 12 characters
- At least one uppercase, one lowercase, one digit, one special character
- Checked via regex in `core/security.py`

### JWT
- Algorithm: HS256 (secret from env var, never hardcoded)
- Access token payload: `sub` (user_id), `role`, `exp`, `iat`
- Refresh tokens stored as hashed value in Redis with TTL

### Role-Based Access Control
- FastAPI dependency `require_role(*roles)` applied per router
- Role matrix:

| Endpoint Group | admin | instructor | learner | finance | dataops |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Locations/Rooms CRUD | ✓ | | | | |
| Sessions CRUD | ✓ | own only | | | |
| Bookings | ✓ | read | own only | read | |
| Checkout/Orders | ✓ | | own only | ✓ | |
| Payments/Refunds | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Promotions CRUD | ✓ | | | ✓ | |
| Search & Export | ✓ | ✓ | | ✓ | |
| Ingestion Config | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Job Monitoring | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
| Audit Logs | ✓ | | | | |
